response.justification: The candidate meets the minimum bar on paper — a bachelor's degree in computer science and roughly 15+ years of hands-on software development across web, mobile, embedded, and high-throughput backend systems — but the profile is that of a strong senior/staff-level implementer, not a director-level principal engineer driving AI strategy. AI exposure is recent and tooling-oriented (MCP code generators, agentic workflow support) rather than recognized expertise with demonstrated AI products or platforms, and there is no evidence of advising strategic customers, shaping cloud product direction, or influencing at the organizational level this role demands.
strengths: 15+ years of broad engineering experience spanning web, mobile, embedded, and low-latency distributed systems
Entrepreneurial track record as founder of an open-source geo-data platform with real-world adoption
Strong craftsmanship credentials: TDD, open-source libraries, workshops, and conference talks
concerns: No demonstrated director/principal-level scope or engineering leadership of large teams or strategy
AI experience is limited to recent agent tooling work, not recognized AI expertise or shipped AI products
No advanced degree in an AI-related field and limited evidence of cloud platform strategy work

key_factors.factor: Lack of AI depth and recognized AI expertise
Seniority gap vs. director-level IC scope
Long, broad engineering experience meeting minimum qualifications
